title: Product development
summary: 9609 A Level — NPD process, concept testing, launch risks, and PLC link.
learningObjectives: Outline stages of new product development.
Explain concept testing and test marketing.
Analyse costs and risks of NPD.
Evaluate NPD strategy for case firms.

simpleExplanation.title: From idea to launch
simpleExplanation.summary: Product development moves from idea generation through screening, concept testing, prototyping, test marketing, and launch — with review against the product life cycle at each stage.
steps: Idea generation and screening — drop weak concepts early.
Prototype build and test marketing in limited area.
Full launch and monitor performance on the PLC.

flashcards.back: Idea generation — R&D, staff suggestions, customer feedback.
Filter ideas by feasibility, cost, fit with brand.
Present idea to sample customers before expensive development.
Limited launch in one region to test real sales.
High R&D cost, failure at launch, cannibalising existing products.
Product development = new product, existing market (8.2.2).
Line extensions cheaper than wholly new products.

sections.content: **Product development** (8.1.2) — case questions often ask **should they launch?** Use **stage-gate logic**: what evidence exists from **concept/test marketing**?

sections.question: Snack firm spent $2m developing a healthy crisps line. Test marketing in two cities showed 60% trial but only 15% repeat purchase. Recommend whether to launch nationally.
sections.solution:
**Positive:** High **trial** — **promotion and packaging** work.

**Negative:** Low **repeat purchase** — ** taste or price** problem; **60% trial wasted** without loyalty.

**Financial risk:** National launch **marketing $5m+** on weak retention → **loss**.

**Recommendation:** **Do not launch yet** — **reformulate** or **reprice**; second test market. **Sunk cost fallacy** — ignore $2m when deciding forward.
